coreTruthSet.description: Comanche are framed as a disruptive force whose full-plains mobility can rewrite existing system assumptions.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Comanche ... disruptive force that rewrites the system ... full plains mobility.

coreTruthSet.description: Osage are represented as dominant range holders using trade and conflict assertively to reinforce territorial control.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Osage ... dominant range holders ... trade used to assert control and reinforce dominance.

coreTruthSet.description: Narrative phase shifts from building influence to sustaining authority under escalating external stress.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Story shifts from building power to holding power under stress.

coreTruthSet.description: Wichita, Osage, and Comanche are framed as pressure systems that test existing network stability rather than ordinary participants.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Wichita -> Osage -> Comanche ... They are pressure systems.

coreTruthSet.description: Wichita are described as plains-river hybrids that expand system reach westward through mobile connector trade logic.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Wichita ... Plains-River Hybrid ... They expand the system westward.

settingAnchors.name: Plains-Caddoan Pressure Interface
settingAnchors.description: Strategic interface where mobile plains powers and established Caddoan-network actors generate sustained pressure on trust and control systems.

continuityConstraints.title: Engaging Self-Sufficient Power
continuityConstraints.text: When counterpart powers do not need your network, leverage shifts to boundary management, risk calculus, and selective reciprocity.

continuityConstraints.title: Disruption Adaptation Cycle
continuityConstraints.text: System durability under Comanche-like disruption requires rapid adaptation loops that update movement, trade, and alliance assumptions in real time.

openResearchGaps.title: At what thresholds did Comanche mobility patterns force structural changes in existing alliance and trade systems?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to mark the transition from stress to systemic rewrite in chapter arcs.

openResearchGaps.title: What specific mechanisms did Osage use to enforce dominance across wide territorial ranges?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed for realistic conflict and negotiation stakes under asymmetric power.

openResearchGaps.title: Which route architectures made Wichita connector power structurally distinct from river-centric Caddoan nodes?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to model westward expansion with concrete movement/trade mechanics.
